review_text: 4/5: Good location, bathrooms were clean and pitches were good. Dropping a star because the kitchen felt like one you get at a busy hostel. Always greasy and dirty surfaces, floors etc. Fridges full to bursting and the corners are loaded up with people’s stuff for some reason. Shame because everything else was good.
Great customer service ,friendly ,quiet and relaxing.enjoyed our time here .

Very expensive paid 2,000 for 7 nights.
Cabin is average  need major upgrades, pool was out of order i think we should have been offered a partial refund.
Putting caravans next to cabins was rather annoying.
Uitstekende locatie, schoon en schaduwrijke plekken.

Der Park könnte viel mehr aus sich machen. Es hat kein Spielplatz, obwohl in der Umgebung keiner zu finden ist und es auf dem Campingplatz genug Möglichkeiten da wären um einen zu erstellen. Die Badezimmer waren zum Teil richtig widerlich und beim einen  Lavabo lief das Wasser nur spärlich ab. Beim Pool wurden nur die Siebe geleert, jedoch nicht mit einem Roboter gereinigt. Es lag ein Haarbüschel (Foto) drei Tage da, bis dieser entfernt wurde. Ein Shop war im April 2026 nicht verfügbar. Der Cable Beach ist zu Fuss in 15 Minuten oder per Auto erreichbar.
Loved this place and coming back
Walked in to enquire about a camp site. Met by Karla at the desk who was absolutely fabulous. Nothing was too much trouble, she's a real customer service star. So i booked 😊

Great park friendly and helpful staff.
Would highly recommend
Sehr schön, alles sauber, schöne Sanitäranlagen und nicer pool
Another great stay at RAC best park by far in the area 🫶
Our 2nd stay at this fabulous park during the wet season, so it was lovely and quiet.  Highly recommend, and Karla will bend over backwards to make sure your time here is a memorable one.
January Very quite this time of year,

Friendly staff
Kitchen good size fridge.
Comfortable bed.

Top Notch Holiday Park|Ticked all the boxes|Just a stop over to look at Cable Beach and restocking in Broome. Highly recommend this park everything was 10/10 not one thing out of place, polite staff, clean amenities and great pool. Also best burger and fish and chips at the little cafe out the front. Walked to the beach to watch the sunset. ✅✅✅
Great park with large caravan bays, the only downside was the pool closed at 6pm or after dark which meant very hot evenings without the chance of cooling down.

I’ve been with RAC insurance for a few years now and, luckily, haven’t had any accidents. I mostly kept my membership for the 4-cent fuel discount… but after recently seeing more of the actual benefits of being an RAC member, I’m now genuinely proud to be part of it (turns out it’s good for more than just cheap fuel). Definitely recommend.
Great location & amenities, fantastic customer service from the lovely Karla who was so helpful & ensured our stay was memorable & enjoyable

During our travels this year we have stayed at 27 different caravan parks and 53 free or low cost off grid camps, most of which have been in WA. We have stayed at 4 RAC caravan parks and this unfortunately this one was disappointing.
The pool was lovely, the staff on arrival was welcoming (and we opted to arrive outside the mayhem time of 11:30am during their peak period of June/July) so that was not stressful.
The sites are a little tight, fortunately ours was larger than most.
The disappointing aspect was the way the park manager spoke to us and other travellers we met whilst staying 7 days in the busy peak season. Monica would benefit from some training in customer service on how to speak to those on holiday. If we break the rules (there is a long list of dos and don’ts) please remind us in a friendly tone, as travellers do not deliberately break them. She yelled at me and she said we would be black listed if she saw me break them again.
Despite this, the caravan park is well located but on our return to Broome we opted to go to another caravan park where service is friendlier.

This place felt crowded because there are camping vans, tents, and cabins all parked together in a small area. The cabins were standard,  the place is well maintained, and is a favorite family camping spot. It took some fiddling to get the air con working optimally. The resort  internet worked outside only. The pool was great! I was happy for the clothesline on the side of the cabin. One night we had some guy sleeping in a one man tent between our parking space and the next camp space. He disappeared before the office opened.

Bit of a luck dip with the reception staff you get here the lady with the purple hair has no customer service skills whatsoever we had booked two sites that we went and had a look at and we locked them in (which you can) come booking in day they had been given away our site’s fair enough but she  had no intentions whatsoever to move us to another shady site, which there was plenty of
We will stay at Cable Beach CP where the staff are much nicer next time 😎

I honestly can’t believe how terrible the customer service was at RAC Cable Beach Holiday Park in Broome. The woman named Maurice, who said she was the manager, was unbelievably rude, condescending, and completely unprofessional.

From the moment we spoke, her tone and attitude was shocking no respect, no basic courtesy, and definitely no sense of how to deal with paying guests. It’s embarrassing that someone like that is representing a place under the RAC name.

If this is how management treats people, it says a lot about how little they care about their customers. Because of how poorly I was spoken to, I decided to stay elsewhere for the night with my workers and I’m glad I did.

Absolutely disgraceful behaviour and a terrible reflection on RAC.

I’ve never experienced such poor customer service as I did with Maurice from RAC Cable Beach Holiday Park, in Broome.
She was extremely rude — it was honestly very off-putting. She claimed to be the manager, which is quite disappointing, as her attitude is a terrible reflection on the business.

I’d strongly suggest staying elsewhere to avoid the unpleasant experience of dealing with her.

Great location
Fabulous staff
Good facilities
Sites are a bit tight, packed in
Excellent venue with great atmosphere 👌

Where to start. Came in and was given a dog friendly site. 2 days later we were summoned like school kids and accused of sneaky our dog in even though its dog friendly. Had to pack up in 37C heat and move mere yards away and set up again in the dog section.

Got the site wrong and were ordered in no uncertain terms to move again despite being an abundance of empty sites all around. The excuse given was that someone had ordered that exact site and comes every year .

Like an idiiot I packed upagain in the heat and moved
I spoke to the person who arrived to take our site and he told me he didnt ask for that site as he doesnt like dogs and didnt care where  he went.
It seems to me like a particular staff member likes playing games with people.

2 of the ladies were very nice but one was rude I wont mention her name. Otherwise the park has excellent facilities

A very bad experience

We stayed for one night in a cabin. The staff was very friendly. The cabin was outdated, the kitchen was very greasy, especially the stove, the cutlery, the plates… The fridge was extremely dirty, there was a lot of sand in it. The bathroom also lacked a minimum level of cleanliness. We didn’t feel very comfortable and would not recommend staying there for longer.
We were in the caravan park. Very clean amenities and the grounds. Very helpful staff and pleasant.

RAC Cable Beach House is an outstanding location with easy access to the beach - perfect for enjoying Broome's stunning sunsets and relaxed atmosphere. The park is well maintained and offers great facilities. It can get very busy, particularly during peak season, and is a favourite among families with young children, so expect a lively environment. A great choice for RAC members seeking a classic Broome holiday experience!

Service was good.  Bed and pillows comfy except scratchy blanket.  Very uncomfortable bathroom door opens inwards making it hard to access.
Shower was not clean, pink mould all over it.
You can see right into next cabin if you open the blind.
The resort was very quiet, nice pool.  Has a parking spot.

We arrived late and missed a night due to a broke down car. When we walked in to the reception to check-in, the amazing lady at the front desk recognized we had been through it and was up for a chat. She then moved our missed night to the end of our stay free of charge. Simple act of kindness made our day and gave a fairly rough 48 hours a positive ending. Amazing customer service - to go along with the nice facilities and great spot. When we return this will be our only go-to. Thanks guys.

A lovely caravan park in Broome with an amazing location right across the street from Cable Beach. Park is quite large with a variety of options to chose from for lodging. We stayed in a 1 bed caravan, and had a nice spacious room with small kitchenette. Room was very clean. Park staff was very friendly and accommodating. Do take note of check in hours as reception is not 24/7. There was a swimming pool onsite and putt putt golf. Price was expensive but was ok for the amazing location. Would recommend when in Broome!
Great park, nice pool and fantastic location. Could do with upgrades to the shower and toilet areas to bring up to the same standard as Karri Valley and Monkey Mia.

On entering I thought - wow, smells clean. Sheets/bed were clean and fresh, kitchen was dirty... dirty microfibre  left in the sink, sink was dirty. None, I repeat NONE of the windows locked, this was despite a warning from the park itself that crime was a huge problem. Yeah - didn't feel safe as a result. Maybe don't send park guests that email because it creates stress? Would I stay there again? Not if Broome Vacation Village was available. Spent 2 nights there before venturing out to camping and they were way better
A very squishy park tight caravan sites. Amenities excellent.

We lilly and Skye, 2 labradors, are travelling around Australia with mum and dad. We stayed here for 5 nights. We all love this place. We had a big site and great neighbours who had 3 furry friends and we loved each other. We could walk to the beach every day. Yay. The beach is our happy place. We will come again one day.

Follow us...next stop Pardoo Station

Overall experience was ok BUT the shower was in POOR condition with MOULD around the base and water damage in the walls. Concerns with HEALTH and Safety in regards to getting sick from the mould… NOT GOOD for the price pay!!

We loved our stay here. Such a great location, in walking distance of Cable Beach to watch the sunset.
The amenities were great - the cleanest and best showers/toilets so far on our roadtrip. Staff were all lovely and friendly and the whole site was immaculate.

We absolutely love this Caravan Park. Just stayed over two weeks and could not fault anything. Pool wonderful - feels like a resort. Amenities very clean. Beautiful Cable Beach across the road.  Staff are exceptional - always helpful. Second time staying here and we will return. No better caravan park in Broome. Thank you 😊

Great park close to Cable Beach and Divers Hotel.  About a 400m walk to the patrolled area of Cable Beach
Sites and the roads around the park are narrow, so mention if you have a bigger rig when you book.
Great pool area, clean facilities and $4 per wash in the laundry.
Office staff are friendly and helpful and the outdoor team are constantly cleaning and maintaining.
The cafe next to the park is ok for a quick and simple snack but look elsewhere if you want something more substantial
Definately recommended

Good amenities.
No unpowered camp sites.
Very noisy at night from traffic of surrounding roads.
Too expensive. $65:00 for a sandy dusty campsite.
Nice and quiet place for staying at Broome.

This place is so cool! Just across from the local pub, The Divers Tavern. And 5 minutes from the iconic Cable Beach. And the local bus system, which is amazing, is just across the road, which goes right into china town et al.

We absolutely loved our stay here! The big amenities block is stunning & the grounds are kept super clean. The pool although is fresh is nice to swim & relax on a hot day. The staff are all so lovely & helpful. Also in the best location with only a short walk or drive to the beach. Everything is only a 10min drive away. Would recommend this is the best caravan park in Broome!
Excellent caravan park. Great amenities and pool. Can walk through dunes to Cable Beach and store and coffee Cafe next door. Divers Tavern over the road.
Nice size. Bathroom and kitchen needs attention.  A lot is forgiven for the washing machine.
Great park. Not far to walk to the beach.
Lovely park. Good shade trees. Good amenities. Close access to Cable Beach
Great spot just across the road from the famous cabel beach nice park
Stayed at the caravan park and enjoyed it

Great Holiday Park.
Toilettes and Bathroom are very clean !

Stayed here for 4 nights in a studio cabin expecting value for money accommodation or at the very least a clean well run facility. The reverse was what we encountered. The cabin was dirty with strands of long black hair clinging to the bathroom floor, dirty kitchen utensils, etc and noisy (backing onto busy Cable Beach Rd) and the dulcet tones of late night music emanating frombthenpub on the corner. It lacks enough accessible and usable amenities such as laundry facilities and wifi. The staff were disinterested at best. We paid $1400 for 4 nights.  My recommendation would be to spend a bit extra and get something decent.

Far better than the larger caravan park just down the road (Cable beach caravan park). Tidy sites, albeit tight are neat. Decent kind of clientele, though I do think the pool pavers could use some attention as looks old and grotty. Definitely nicer than the alternatives (staying at Cable Beach van park now, it's derelict and completely different vibe). Van park at town beach has too many problems with crime and undesirables. Terrific beach area here.

Just to touch on van site sizes - the roads are narrow, which is the difficult part in terms of menouvering. Sites are sufficient, but our neighbour just for eg had to get the neighbours across from him to move their car out so he could move his van over in preparation for leaving the next day otherwise he was stuck due to a fence next door (rear of van would have struck it). You want to be pretty confident and competent booking in here, beginner/novices it may be a bit much. Many sites you need help from neighbours to get in or out because the angle is impossible with them parked where they are. Some trees pose a terrific obstacle course too and really serve no purpose being where they are. In saying that, this is the best place to park up in Broome. You need to book far in advance because most regular visitors know they do NOT want to have to be in the other parks.
Tight spaces to get your caravan in or out
Such a beautiful place staff were amazing clean and tidy facilities and so friendly
Very busy park, good size caravan sites. Close to cable beach. Very clean and modern amenities. Quite expensive $80 per night for a powered site.
Amazing spot

My wife had an accident & I needed a site that afternoon. Monica & her staff some how got me in. We had to stay for a month in Broome. Again Monica organised for us to stay on the same site for the month. I can't thank all the  staff enough. Just such caring & friendly people. Wish I could give 10 stars.
Great place, and staff just needs something for kids IE play ground / pillow / adventure  play
A beautiful park with wonderful, clean amenities

We stayed here for 2 weeks with mixed feelings about the park. Firstly we ripped our mud flap off the motorhome trying to get onsite with a huge hole right out the front of our site, the gutters are high enough as it is without having a huge hole right in front of the site.  Nick the groundsman was awesome and filled the hole for us making it so much easier to get on and off site.  Nick was super friendly and was always willing to assist with anything that was needed, taking great care of the park grounds and showing passion for the job he does.    |The noise from the airport doesn’t stop, it goes 24/7 so be prepared.  There is a relatively new amenities block which is lovely but it needs a good scrub to keep it looking new, it is getting dated and stained very quickly.
Monica and team are amazing. They have gone above and beyond with our stay. Many thanks to them for looking after us during such a crazy and busy time. You deserve more than 5 stars.

We stayed in a cabin in this caravan park. The cabin was old and in desperate need of renovation. The fan in the bathroom/laundry was filthy and there were cracked tiles in this area. The rangehood was filthy and didn’t work. The microwave was placed on top of the refrigerator which was way above my head and I would have been too afraid to use it. The location of the park near Cable Beach is good. I did give the actual park a negative review and did get a very positive response from them about addressing my concerns, which I consider extremely promising. If you are in your own caravan, it’s a great spot.

We have enjoyed a fantastic stay at this beautiful caravan park for 5 weeks, the staff are amazing,friendly and most helpful
Special mention to Monica the manager who helped us out from day 1 we were a week early because of unseasonable rains and she was able to accomodate us on the same site for our whole duration that was a great job done Monica.
The reception staff in Karla, and Lotty again so helpful and friendly  when we had a request
The maintenance gents in Nick and Mick just top fellas always moving around the grounds and helping out customers when needed
Lyn and her team of people who look after the cabins always had time for a chat
The parks location is 10/10 close to the beautiful cable beach , Divers tavern,general store and walking distance to restaurants
The amenities are always very clean,nice shady sites on the main and no thorough fare traffic making the experience all the better as no vehicles are driving up and down roads
Very good swimming pool and ambience all leaning to a very well run park and relaxing time had by guests
Our time here was so good we have booked again for next year 10/10 from us Peter and Bev Perth
Staff very friendly and switched on. Site was large enough for van annex and silverado. Amenities were awesome. Clean plenty of showers and toilets very modern. Loved the stay.

The staff at RAC were great. Especially Carla, who went above and beyond to help with room changes, facilities and even a lift to the airport when taxis were booked out! If you’re in Broome, RAC caravan park is your stop 👍🏻

Location within walking distance near Cable Beach and opposite a bus stop. Staff were friendly. Cabin generally clean but some amenities (bath shower and window locks) are a little old. Near General Store which is convenient.
Very happy with the park. Friendly staff, clean amenities and great sites.

Very busy Caravan park with good amenities, nice pool and camp kitchen. Camp sites are a bit tight in areas, however this is an older style park built prior to the big van era. We are very happy with our choice, following recommendations from friends.

Great location and a very well maintained caravan park. The staff were very friendly and helpful 🙂 I would always stay here when in Broom. The only negative is that you can only check in and out on Mondays and Fridays, which is not always possible.

We absolutely loved our stay here! From the moment we arrived, the staff were friendly, welcoming, and incredibly helpful. A special mention to Manager Monica – she was amazing!  We didn’t have levelling ramps, but Monica went above and beyond, asking her staff, and Michael kindly lent us his. Monica also helped extend our stay without any hassle. The whole place was cozy, clean, and spotless – truly well-maintained throughout.|The pool was fantastic, complete with an adjoining toilet and shower. The camp kitchen is hands down the best we’ve ever come across – with a large freezer, a see-through fridge, and everything you could possibly need. The laundry and drying facilities were perfect too.|The location is excellent – close to beautiful Cable Beach and right next to a General Store that offers daily amenities, great hot food, with dinner specials.|As someone who’s cautious around dogs, I really appreciated that they enforce leash rules – it made for a much more comfortable and relaxed stay.|Highly recommend – we’ll be back!

Fantastic Stay in Our Sprinter Campervan 4x4
We absolutely loved our stay here! From the moment we arrived, the staff were friendly, welcoming, and incredibly helpful. A special mention to Manager Monica – she was amazing!  We didn’t have levelling ramps, but Monica went above and beyond, asking her staff, and Michael kindly lent us his. Monica also helped extend our stay without any hassle. The whole place was cozy, clean, and spotless – truly well-maintained throughout.
The pool was fantastic, complete with an adjoining toilet and shower. The camp kitchen is hands down the best we’ve ever come across – with a large freezer, a see-through fridge, and everything you could possibly need. The laundry and drying facilities were perfect too.
The location is excellent – close to beautiful Cable Beach and right next to a General Store that offers daily amenities, great hot food, with dinner specials.
As someone who’s cautious around dogs, I really appreciated that they enforce leash rules – it made for a much more comfortable and relaxed stay.
Highly recommend – we’ll be back!
Thanks Kate & Per
Nice park, clean amenities, but it was tight to manoeuvre caravan into the bays

Broome is a beautiful location to enjoy the peace of northern WA, I’m traveling with my 14yo service dog to visit sick kids in hospitals, small breed well trained Corgie X, this morning whilst in my paid camp area 3 feet from my van my dog is sitting down when the park Karan that I’ve never seen warns me on fear of evection from the park that she needs to be on a leash no matter where!|would not listen to why she’s not on a leash because of a neck injury just wanted to be high and mighty at 7am|later found out from other locals that they hate dogs and will look for any excuse to start an argument!|so all you dog lovers just beware of the rude, threatening and hateful attitude of people who don’t like dogs. |Policy or not they could have just let me explain, i will never return to this park again. |Dog lovers beware!!
We had a cabin and it was great.

On a positive - the amenities here are very good. Sites allocated to rooftop and slide on campers, however, are unfit for purpose. Too small and in the worst locations in the park and yet charged at the same rate as sites more than twice as large with better amenity and privacy.
Small campers and slide-ons are advised to bypass this park as you’re simply subsidising those with big caravans and super sized cars. We had similar experiences in other RAC parks and expected better. Lesson learned.

We were really looking forward to our stay at the RAC park Cable Beach as we had been traveling for over 15 months and were meeting family and friends in Broome for birthday celebrations and had stayed there on 3 previous occasions. Our booking was made 11 months in advance and one of the reasons for choosing RAC was because we are members. That doesn’t mean much. We were allocated a site right down the back of the park against the fence which was too small & when we requested a different location we were told that it was not possible because that meant they would have to change a lot of other bookings. We were advised to just go in on an angle & that will do. However, during our 9-night stay, we noticed quite a few big sites occupied by smaller vans & rooftop tents & camper trailers which turned over regularly. Another point, of which I realize the park can’t control but to note, there is a lot of noise from the airport. We would consider another CP when visiting Broome in the future.

This was a terrible park! We only have a small campervan and we could not even put our chairs and table beside the van it was so small. Could not even open the driver door as there was a tree in the way. We had a view of the dump site and the tanks and backed on to the noisy camp kitchen! The other sites also looked cramped. The staff were helpful though!

Great park. Highlights are the location, within walking distance of taverns and the beach. And the pool. Best kept pool at a caravan park we’ve visited yet. The site was ok. The layout is a little odd but gives the park some character I guess. The ablutions were brand new and top notch, no complaints there. We had a text warning to lock up our stuff due to increased crime in Broome. There was also supposed to be Security patrols in the park but I didn’t notice any (not to say there wasn’t). Anyway, we didn’t have any problems. The text was the only worrying bit of the stay 😒. We’re were very happy overall and would stay there again.

Arrived with a caravan for a 12 day stay. Requested a specific bay at check in but was sternly told 'we are fully booked' when we noticed plenty of empty bays. We were given a site that was unshaded and full sun most of the day. The bay I asked about on check in, that was a shady site, was empty for 2 days then a Maui van pulled in for a couple of nights, bay empty again for a day, then a caravan arrived. We were very disappointed that we werent allowed that particular bay? After being told that bays can't be reserved when originally booking I don't understand why we weren't given the option of a bay that was vacant on our arrival especially since we were staying for 12 days. I heard a staff member telling a guest that if they were an RAC member they can request a specific bay. I have been a member for 48 years and was NOT given any special consideration.
The park states it is pet friendly but there are NO dog poo bags anywhere in the park. Luckily I arrived with plenty in tow.
The park states it is smoke free, however we saw several people smoking on the grounds and other guests at the park make remarks about those rules not being followed.
The park was busy and the ablutions where not a nice place to visit. Since there where so many people at the park it would have been appropriate for more frequent cleaning of the ablutions.
The bays are not easy to get into, very tight, and many people where of the same opinion as us.
The roads within the park had a high build up of sand and stones and where in dire need of a road sweep.
A very disappointing experience especially since we had such an awful unshaded site when we asked for something different on arrival. For an RAC caravan park the welcoming at check in was not as friendly as we would have expected. Can't think of one good thing to say about the park, would never recommend it to anyone and needless to say will never return.

Would we stay again? NO
Firstly. The staff were very friendly and tried to be helpful when they could.Amenities block was fantastic. Great layout and always  clean.
Be prepared for loud Airport noise from 5am.
But in 20 years of Caravaning we have never come across a park that was so difficult to manoeuvre. Once inside  the roads are barely  wider  than a van. If you meet another  car coming towards  you, you can not pass.  One has to reverse.  The internal roadway system is an absolute mess. The site roads are all dead end. Road width are narrower than a dual cab ute. Near impossible to reverse In as opposite sites have vans with Draw bars to the curb

This park needs to sacrafice some sites to allow   leaving the streets open both ends.
Staff need to police how cars are parked.  Didn't help site opposite had a long van  and a Boat and trailer, leaving their car half on road.  They also need to ensure long vans one side and short  vans the other.

It took 20 minutes of manoeuvring  to even get out of the site. Hookup was with car on a 35 deg angle. You can not back up square on to hook up.

Didn't  notice the similar  reviews b4 hand. It appears  they are aware of the issues but have taken no actions to correct.
This layout  caused a great deal of stress in an otherwise ideal place. A shame really.

The staff were very helpful and friendly, the cabin had everything you could
need and most importantly the AC was ice cold:)
Good park with a great swimming pool, clean facilities.Only 500 metre walk to Cable Beach.Well stocked general store next door with PuttPutt. Hotel across the road . 5 minute drive into town.
We have stayed here before and the staff and facilities were great. Second stay, new management are unwelcoming and standard of maintenance has declined. Won't be coming back.

Convenient , the pool was nice. But the planning for the van sites needs serious attention. As a RAC member I thought we would of got perhaps a little better consideration. The spot given to us was like a gravel pit at the very back of 2 cabins. Little room, no shade what so ever & tight for room. In the 5 days we were there we noticed "many" grassed shaded spots around the park that were empty. We intended to stay a little longer in Broome - but couldn't wait to get out of that park. For us - that's twice. First & last time !!!. I could go on about it all, but to no value really. But certainly - never again !!!.
They have a convenience store with a post box, a couple of pools and communal kitchen,powered sites, many happy customers returning

Great place to stay
Kids and dog friendly
Great facilities
Everyone seems friendly, you camp next too
Had a great experience
Little inflexible with van spot we were in for a long term holiday. A few issues including safety weren't enough.
Great pool and huge sites with heaps of shade. Just remember to lock your car at night as we had someone go through ours one night.

I gave this a 1 as the staff were polite, compassionate and understanding.  The caravan park was diabolical.  Highlight was the lane in the park that was too narrow for vans.  It had a sign on one end but not the other saying “no vans”.  Only problem was you had to drive down it to park in our site.

Stay here if you like advanced driving courses.  Stay here if you have already smashed every panel on your car and van and are looking for an insurance claim.  Stay here if you have a chainsaw and can cut down a few trees and the fence.  Stay in their biggest sites only if you have a camper trailer.

We left after two of the staff acknowledged our site was untenable.  Moved down the road to the Discovery Park and got a big grassed site that was cheaper.

We have been impressed by the RAC parks in the past, however I’d suggest this one needs to review the size of its sites and the road access to them.

We were grateful to have the $73 a night we had paid refunded.

I walked in to book and paid for a cabin in advance, giving a Victoria post code. I didn’t have my RAC membership number readily available. The next day when I checked in I was told it would cost another $70 as i was not a WA member. It was hot and I was travelling by motorcycle so I agreed to pay the extra. Whilst waiting for my key I checked booking.com and the rate was cheaper than the membership rate. I again questioned why I was being charged extra when the previous day I had given a Vic address. I suggested they honour the original price paid or at least match the booking.com price. They refused. They did offer a total refund however but the $520 I had paid would not be released back to my account for 21 days by my CC company. The promised early check in if the room was available never happened. No one gets to check in before 2pm. The reception staff run it in a military fashion. The accommodation was acceptable but drab and totally overpriced. The environment is depressing. There are much better options available with real customer service, far cheaper. Avoid this place!!
Stayed in our own van on a powered site. Close to beach and restaurants.  Pool and amenities are clean, staff are lovely

Everything you need, bus outside, pub across road, gemeral store cafe onsite and lush pool. Reception and security ladies are wonderful. Stress free and so relaxing and homely....very happy camper I am

LOVE this place!!! We initially dropped off the caravan for a service in Broome, thinking we would get that done and keep heading south but then hubby said why don't we stay in Broome for a day or 2, lets check out a couple of caravan parks and see what they have......first stop was here and the welcome we got was amazing, such friendly staff....even got the usual disparaging comment on hubbys choice of Carlton Footy number plates! :-) 😂 We felt welcome from the minute we arrived.....Jacqui suggested we have a look around the park for the best spot for us to park up, half the park was closed for maintenance but there were still many options at this time of year and we found the spot we wanted so booked for a couple of nights.....definitely no need to check out any other parks!

First day hubby met Todd and I swear they were a match made in heaven.....definitely 2 peas in a pod! Todd was always racing around but always had time to stop and chat to all the guests, albeit the weather or the temperature of the pool! Oh yeah, the pool is awesome, so relaxing, sometimes the temp was too hot but it was still nice to chill out listening to the local radio!

In the end we booked for an extra week, too nice and very comfortable! Highly recommend this place.... we even had breakfast twice at the Cafe adjoined to the park. Overall I give 100% (We did talk to other amazing folks/workers here but I have forgotten your names sorry....the guy I chatted to about Carnarvon and the lovely girl in the office when I booked for an extra week).

Todd kept us updated with the possible cyclone coming in and had great suggestions for places to stay south of here, what a gem he is!

The park is amazing, clean ablutions, great sites, shady spots, friendly staff! Todd us an asset...don't let him go.

Reshuffling our travel itinerary due to 3x extended stay, we do love Broome and this caravan park. First of all, Hayden and Tod are beyond helpful, working around the clock making sure, we had a wonderful stay. They also kept us updated re weather, roads and local attractions.
We spent long periods of time in the salt water swimming pool, which ended up being the daily meeting place. All amenities were super clean and looked brand new, the sites were shady with trees scattered around.
The caravan park had a great vibe and really fun people around.
Cable beach is just over the dune and the local Divers Tavern is also just in front of you.
Leaving with a heavy heart but great memories. Thank you, we will be back!
Very nice hotel,

Place de camping avec électricité. Réservé la veille !!  Le meilleur camping depuis une semaine sur place !! Les sanitaires et laundry sont d'un blanc immaculé, propre, lumineux. Cerise sur le gâteau il y a même de la musique !! La piscine est excellente !! Il y a à 3 min à pied un mini golf, petite supérette possibilité de manger sur place des plats, burger, fish& chips, sandwichs, burgers ...juste à traverser la route pour les boissons alcoolisées.

Wunderschöner Platz, tolle sanitäre Anlagen.  Der Park verfügt über einen kleinen Laden und ist toll gelegen. Der Cable Beach ist zu Fuss in 10 Minuten erreichbar. Das Personal ist ausserordentlich freundlich. Wir hatten es sehr ruhig da ausser Saison.
Great to stay with room to park caravan,  staff great.

We stayed here with a car and two swags. Everyone of the staff was soooo friendly and helped us with what we could do in the area and where we could go next. The location is perfect, near cable beach, right next to a busstop, and close to cafés and restaurants. There’s a little shop next to the camping where they have good coffee. Thanks again to the staff for our lovely stay!
Lovely campsite and the owners were very friendly.  Ended up staying a few extra nights !

We stayed this week (17/11/24) for a couple of days in a camper, lovely site we arrived late after closing and they kindly left a welcome pack for us to collect with email confirmation, very friendly and helpful hosts well recommended.
Close to the beach and general store ,
Thanks for a great stay

Wonderful, helpful and friendly staff.
Can't thank them enough for all they did whilst I was staying with them.
Beautiful pool, lovely cabin and so close to the beach. A little peice of paradise

Have stayed here many times over the years on our trips to Broome from Derby and always in the same cabins nearest the pool.
We had requested an early check in as it's off season we knew it wouldn't be too busy but we were told the park was too busy.
Unfortunately this time we had an older cabin toward the back of the park, unsure why as the park was quiet and cabins not occupied nearer the pool.
Took a long time for the aircons to kick in and cool the cabin down as all aircons but one were older style.
It would have been nice if aircons had have been turned on a few hours earlier prior to check in time and considering the high temperature this time of year. The cabin hadn't been cleaned recently, floor was dirty. Very noisy creaky cabin with something banging, dropping on the roof constantly. The bed was uncomfortable, was like being in a water bed.
We didn't get much sleep at all.
Next time we will ensure we are in the nicer cabins near the pool.

every caravan park needs a "Todd" - what an absolute legend! I was hot, tired and cranky due to power going out during the night and the number of kids that were bombing the pool so couldn't enjoy it at all!  I don't mind kids - have my own, but wish some parents would teach them NOT to jump so close to others...
Next day hubby mentioned the power to Todd who was awesome! moved us to another site, helped us move, followed up to see how we were doing - turned our whole stay around! :)
wifi is also good enough for me to work from - first time that has happened

We stayed at the RAC during the dry season and were fortunate to get a spot, as most sites are pre-booked every year by the same grey nomads for the entire dry season (June-August). Many of them start leaving around September as the heat sets in, heading south again. Personally, I don't think this should be allowed, considering the remote location. It's frustrating to see sites locked down for such long periods when many families with small children or pets are left having to stay at the overflow camp (pistol club) with limited powered sites and no air conditioning. They miss out on truly experiencing Broome because of this.

The caravan park itself is probably the cleanest in the area, and its location is fantastic – just a 400m walk to Cable Beach’s main area or 200m over the sand dune.

The grounds staff were wonderful. Unfortunately, one lady in the office really let the team down. After multiple rude interactions, I realized it wasn’t just a bad day – it was her usual attitude.

The park is incredibly busy and fully booked, so you end up feeling like just another number. If you leave unhappy, it doesn't seem to matter much, as they’ll easily fill your site with the next person.

Having done a full lap of Australia and stayed at over 100 caravan parks, this was sadly the worst customer service we’ve experienced. Many other guests shared the same sentiment about this one particular office staff member. When we raised a complaint, the manager was apologetic but made excuses for the staff member's behaviour.

On the positive side, the sites are large and shady, and the amenities are modern and clean. Leaves do tend to gather on the sites, but the groundsmen, especially Lee, do a fantastic job cleaning up daily. Lee, in particular, was a true gentleman – always helpful, with a friendly hello and a smile. He's definitely an asset to the park.

From the moment we arrived at RAC Cable Beach we were greeted by the lovely staff and could go and pick our site as it was coming out of peak season.

The park was clean, very presentable and the groundsman Todd greeted us everyday with a smile and chat about some great places to visit on our travels.

The toilets, showers and camp kitchen were very clean and our site 137 was spacious and the pool was so refreshing to jump into with sun chairs and plenty of shade to relax and read your kindle.

And the other best part was we were a 2 minute drive from the beach where we would meet mates, swim, have some drinks and dinner and watch the beautiful sunset which never got old.

We only booked a week, but we re booked twice as we absolutely loved it there and didn't want to leave.
Great food at the cafe. Didn't stay at the park. The owner shouldn't yell at staff when customers are there, though.
Used Caravan sites. Amenities we clean and tidy. Friendly staff, good park. Bit expensive.

Called in to reception grab a quote for a long term stay 2 months the lady who served me disused sites and rates and wrote the price down on the site map  so we walked through the park picked what site we thought would suit us upon returning the next day  a different lady was at the counter and very very Rude l might add quote me a different price which was different? So I explained to her what happened the day before but she wasn’t interested stating that what the previous lady had quoted was incorrect? So all I would say to all that book in to this park is to SHOP AROUND as there are better places and PRICES and you Will NOT have to deal with this RUDE PERSON
We only stayed one night but were very happy with this park.

Warm welcoming staff, we were a last minute check-in both times in September and they had room and availability to extend our stay. Great location to Cable beach and the beach can be accessed just across the road or short drive to the main part/patrolled beach near the camel rides. Walking distance to restaurants too.
The park itself had wildlife blue tounge, tata and frilled neck lizards.
We had the choice of sites for our stay, great new amenities, nice well maintained pool and gardens.

We opted to stay at RAC Cable Beach because of it's access to Cable Beach. It was a great spot with amazing new bathrooms, pool and access to Divers Tavern. Staff were super helpful and friendly too. If you are wanting to stay slightly out of town this is the place for you!
Can’t get Cell service and wifi not working
Lack of laundry facilities
Great park. Good amenities.

Very crowded and squished in like sardines, the tightest sites we've seen in our 5 and a half years of travelling around Australia - our neighbours can't even open their awning. Crusher dust is the ground cover so not overly nice. The amenities are reasonably new but there's not enough of them for a park this size, they're not cleaned particularly well and in need of a good scrub. Some of the staff are friendly and helpful and some are not. There are some beautiful trees in the park and in my opinion the most redeeming feature of the park. We came here because of the swimming pool but didn't get to have a swim as travelling families with their children took over the pool every day and it's not a very large pool so wouldn't have been overly enjoyable. Wouldn't stay here again.

Thanks to all the staff RAC Cable beach caravan Park.
We checked in on the 11th of may and stayed until  the 18th of July.
During this time I became ill and had to spend some time in hospital.
While I was in hospital and afterwards all of the staff went the extra mile to take care of us. Many Many thanks.
We have been visiting this park for the last  10 years and are booked again for next year.
I can see money has been spent on upgrading the facilities and things in general.
Would  well recommend staying here.
100% to you guys.
We were in Broome, and want to go back next year with our caravan. We went to check out the caravan park. It was neat and tidy, good facilities and friendly staff. It's walking distance to the beach.

A bit dusty
Pool not heated

The staff at this caravan park were unbelievable. They went above and beyond when we had vehicle issues recently. Liz in the office found us a place to stay each time I went in and managed to keep us on the same site.  Todd, Murray and Hayden were incredible in their help and support whilst were there, with storage and assistance and just a friendly chat.
The caravan park is also amazing great pool and amenities
Well done and thanks RAC Cable Beach Caravan Park

Had 3 nights in July. Powered site. Think we lucked out with a nice shady corner site opposite the really nice toilet block. Close to Cable beach and the pubs inbetween so no need to drive. Be aware when arriving Monday or Friday as its absolute chaos getting in even though they let you in at 11.30.
Great service, all the staff have been very helpful.

Escorted into caravan site by staff member,who were also friendly when spotted during stay.
Excellent amenities with overhead fans,tiled floor,heaps of washing machines.
Shaded caravan sites.
Clean,well kept pool area.
Close to General Store,Tavern,Bus Stop & 500mtrs to beach.Dog friendly.
Very packed in sites, great location to  access cable beach
We had a great site near the pool. Full park whilst we were there, so very busy at all the amenities.

We spent 3 nights in June 2024 in a caravan site at RAC Cable Beach Holiday Park.  We had a great time here.  There are many shady trees within the park and the amenities look like they have just been renewed.  The swimming pool area is beautiful. The staff at Reception are very friendly and efficient.  The sites are a bit tight to get into, but once your in it's fine.

Nice enough caravan park, but oddly shaped and luck of the draw as to where you are. Only one main set of amenities (the other is small and old) and one laundry, which is clear across the park from some of the campsites. Should be nicer for the cost, but the location is good - walking distance to beach, cafe and restaurants.
Wonderful experience

Wonderful park! Stayed 5 nights in our camper. The staff were incredibly helpful and friendly. Beautiful and modern amenities. Close to the beach and really enjoyed swimming in the pool!  Would highly recommend this park 😃

Very cramped, very noise (airport, road traffic and park traffic, blower going at 7am to clean the mini putt course each morning) Bright flood lights on until 10pm. The grey water waste drain was bocked the 4 nights we stayed. Pool so chlorinated that you couldn't see the bottom. Not a place you would stay for a break. Purely just a stop over for supplies. $60 for 2 adults on a powered site.

We decided to stay long term in Broome as we had arranged work within the town.  Booked into RAC Caravan Park, Broome and on check in requested to pay weekly.  Advised I was required to pay Fortnightly as ‘Things change’ by the manager.  I thought that was fair enough.  A couple of days later when my rent was due I arrived in reception to be told I needed to pay a month in advance and then needed to pay fortnightly thereafter.  No reason was given other than, ‘that’s what the manager has told us’.  Besides been contrary to RAC terms and conditions, it didn’t make sense, as when It came time to leave, they would owe me two weeks back rent.  After informing reception that I didn’t have enough money to pay for a month they accepted two weeks rent.  Fifteen minutes later reception rang and told me that they had checked with the owners and I would have to pay the full month in advance??!!  I told the receptionist I would  leave after the expiration of the two weeks.  I’m putting it down to a total breakdown in communication between the owner and receptionist.  It did seem that their default position was a lack of trust of customers!  No hard feelings but I wouldn’t say there again.  Pity really!

Good campground right next to the beach. The newly constructed facilities were really nice, the older ones not so much. The park is quite spread out so you might have to go very far to the next toilet. Pool was very nice and refreshing. The airport nearby can get quite loud.
The best park we've stayed in and it's our 83rd. I would have given 5 stars, but the pool was far too noisy with children..not relaxing.
Very clean and tidy chalets. Quiet too. Comfortable bed

Not very accommodating, to locals who refer business there way ,including family for every year of the Gibb River challenge..
Won't be doing this in the future..

Great location in walking distance to cable beach and a handful of eateries.
Amenities and park are definitely showing their age and the caravan park is presumably a work in progress since RAC took over as there is a lot of wear and tear to repair and upgrade to bring it into this century.
Note that the main town is a 10 minute hourly bus ride away which unfortunately stops running just after 6pm, so a $35 taxi ride back if you want to sample many of the fine eateries or attractions there. Hopefully the bus service grows to running later into the evening in the future.

Very nice and tidy caravan park. Five stars. We had a powered site. The ablutions are very clean, partly brand new and equipped with fly nets. Friendly staff.
Beautiful pool area. The management should be far more present to advise parents that the pool is not exclusively a noisy child playground but also a place for customers seeking relaxing enjoyment.
Lovely place to stay very clean and tidy management and staff are very friendly and helpful would recommend a stay here and we will be back thankyou Hayden and Jodie and staff
Cabins were a great option for travelling with children

Der Platz ist sehr sauber und gut geführt. Der Pool war sauber und hat, zumindest in der Nebensaison, genügend Liegestühle. Zu Fuss ist man in knapp 10 Minuten an der Cable Beach. Der Treffpunkt für die Kameltour am Strand ist ebenfalls zu Fuss zu erreichen. Die Bushaltestelle liegt direkt vor den Toren des Campingplatzes. Die andere Seite von Broome kann also ohne eigenes Fahrzeug erreicht werden. |Ein campingeigenes Café und ein kleiner Shop, der das Wichtigste anbietet, öffnet bereits um 6 am. Was für Frühaufsteher, wie uns, ein Segen ist. Der Kaffee ist nämlich hervorragend.|Wir empfehlen den Platz all unseren Freunden, falls ihr Reiseziel irgendwann einmal Broome sein wird.  |Wir waren einen Tag später angereist, als gebucht (Buschfeuer verhinderte uns die Weiterfahrt von 80 mile Beach nach Broome). Der Preis wurde uns entsprechend reduziert, was ich nicht als Selbstverständlichkeit erschte. Herzlichen Dank dafür!
Great place to stay near Cable Beach with powered sites. Nice pool. Friendly staff and dog friendly 🐕🐾

We stayed In a cabin that Sleeps 5 it was perfect for a family and nice and clean and plenty of room. We were booked in to stay for 3 Nights but we had some bad luck with our Vehicle The management at park went above and beyond to help us and at short Notice were able to rearrange things so we could stay in Cabin another night Till we got our Vehicle sorted. We can’t thank them Enough. From Steve and Gina Slack.

What a week we had!
Stayed in our van. The site was a great size and we got lucky with a tree nearby that gave us a nice shade in the afternoons.
Everyone staff member we interacted with was lovely and always willing to help.
And the pool 🙌🏻🙌🏻 what an absolute delight. We swam morning and afternoon. There was plenty of shaded areas around it to sit. The temperature during the hot days was a blessing.
We look forward to coming back again.
Ne changé rien c'est super 👍

Sites large and shady
Bit of a hike to the beach especially having walking track closed for road works
Staff were beautiful
Pool lovely shame it can’t be utilised up until quiet time as temperatures are high
Very surprised not a blade of grass but  not dusty
Toilet blocks all work. Good hot water but rundown in desperate need of a refurb to suit the price of the site per night

해변이 가까워서 산책하기 좋다.
비싸다. 인터넷 거의 불가능.
복잡하다.

Very disappointing ideal as a caravan park and that is all!!! Stayed in a cabin which was situated right at the back of the park road noise day and night awful not ideal as already said needs to be a caravan park only 😡😡😡
Very spacious well appointed 2 bedroom unit. Park very clean and well maintained.  Staff friendly and helpful. Able to book trips from main office. Restaurant and shop available on property
Hayden and Liz have this caravan park operating a high level, they are honest hard working people with a can do attitude. Cheers guys👍

This is a well oiled machine. The booking process was a little difficult but check-in and check out were super efficient. The whole place felt well organised. I think it's probably the nicest caravan park in Broome, although we have only stayed at one other we looked at another couple and they seemed barren by comparison. It's not perfect. The camp kitchen is more or less useless being at one corner of the park. The toilets near us I thought were cramped. Small showers with little place to put your stuff, cramped urinal, small basin area. The place feels quite old and a little tired. However if I was back in Broome I would try here first!
Great staff always ready to accommodate your needs and requests

We had an amazing experience at the RAC Caravan Park. The staff are absolutely delightful and always there to help, just overall really nice and welcoming. The location and amenities are great as well, would absolutely recommend!
Camping is good but very close to airport so if you like early morning then your fine
Amazing staff, Amazing room's just full of old people 😆  pools is great but ocean is just a stones throw away.
Gorgeous park. Excellent facilities. Sites are a bit squishy.

Big caravan park with pool.
Nice shady camp sites and great size.
Facilities are clean.
Just few minutes to cable beach.

Not quite what I expected for the price the staff are great amenities are clean and roomy the park could do with a bit of a make over van sites very dusty some artificial grass or concrete slabs on the van sites would help a lot.

overpriced, old and dirty toilets camp kitchen is small and half functional.. 4g and wifi no working !!
doesn't meet the standard of the other RAC around W.A
been travelling around with RAC Park ( monkey mia, exmouth etc..) this one is the worst from far !
we left on Friday ( because can not even leave during the weekend) but lucky because we booked g'day park for the weekend, it was cheaper and way better !!

Great park, huge pool, lots of shade from the trees which was great since it was so hot, large sites, nice amenities, one small kitchen area so we just cooked at our site, luckily at the front desk I had the two nice young ladies helping me out because the other staff, Jean, “helping” guests was grumpy and had poor customer service etiquette.

Lovely place. They gave us a perfect site.
Some sites are a little squashed.
I can't understand their rules regarding no booking in or out on weekends.
Come on this is the RAC.
Must be the only caravan park in Australia that had such a rule.
I hope it's because of staffing issues.
But the staff are very good and the place is very tidy and clean.
Thanks to the reception team. Very efficient and helpful.
The swimming pool is superb.
The proximity to cable beach makes it worth it to stay over the weekend.
Very tight parks for 14mte plus sites can't get pegs in the ground could be buried bricks or rocks. Gate to kiosk not locked securely as me as adult could get through.

This business pretends to be ‘dog friendly’.

This means: if you have a pet, they put you in a separate area at the back of the caravan park in much smaller sites (but for the same price as a large caravan site).

Their excuse being “because some people don’t like dogs”.

Then…

“Why not have a separate section for people who don’t like dogs (and one for people who don’t like kids too)” I asked - “instead of imposing this on the entire park?”

“Mmmfff” and “rules are rules” was what the grumpy receptionist replied.

It felt like this park was run like a school with the “headmistress” marching around the park checking on people and their “dog friendly” sign is just to get customers as there is nothing friendly about this park, and the staff’s attitude.

So… 1 star for the location, level sites and nice (but freezing) pool…
But zero for the ‘welcome’ (they choose to answer phones in the middle of a face to face conversation with you, at the desk and make you wait like you don’t exist), dislikes of animals and really old and uncomfortable amenities.

We stayed here in a powered site
It was really hard to enjoy my stay at this caravan Park
There is children riding there bike all day with no supervision
Screaming and yelling
As we drive out the children and older children would just ride straight in front of our car
No idea if road rules at all
We found it very stressful
As we were hooking up to leave an older boy did get 2 little kids maybe 3 years old each to follow him and they were so small over the front of the car we couldn't see them
I did hear a lot of ladies in the abolition blocks commenting on how dangerous the children were on there bikes
My recommendation is for management to conduct a road safety for the kids on bikes
So in summary
Do yourself a favour and go to another caravan Park
Honestly it was not relaxing at all
